rag_rules: Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when referring to component ports
Use component implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings
Ensure AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Align helper declaration types with expression types for valid comparisons
generic_rules: Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Remove generated clauses for range comparisons or 0/1 equality over non-numeric data ports
Use `eq range_value : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value;` for valid range comparisons
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid
Use visible identifiers from the current scope
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions and guarantees
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
